name: Theo
context: hospital nurse; threads: night shifts, balcony garden, younger brother's wedding

episodes.text: Came off a stretch of three night shifts. Slept until two, then repotted the rosemary and swept the balcony while the kettle ran.

probes.query: Which herb did I repot after the night shifts?
I feel calm remembering that slow recovery day after nights.
Those daylight hours spent putting my small world in order come back to me.

episodes.text: A patient I had looked after for six weeks was discharged today. Her daughter hugged me at the elevator and I kept my composure until they were out of sight.

probes.query: How long had I looked after the patient who was discharged?
I feel moved and proud whenever that discharge day comes to mind.
A goodbye by the lift doors, held together until it was over, stays with me.

episodes.text: My brother asked me to be his best man over grilled mackerel at our usual place. He rehearsed it like a speech and knocked over the soju pouring too fast.

probes.query: What were we eating when my brother asked me to be best man?
I feel a rush of happiness remembering my brother's clumsy request.
The question he practiced too hard to ask me keeps replaying warmly.

episodes.text: Grocery run and pharmacy pickup, then batch-cooked curry for the freezer. Labeled four containers with dates like the ward taught me.

probes.query: How many curry containers did I label for the freezer?
I feel settled thinking of that practical cooking day.
Rows of neat meals waiting in the cold drift into my head on busy weeks.

episodes.text: We lost a patient on the night shift despite forty minutes of resuscitation. I signed the paperwork, debriefed with the team, and cried in my car before driving home.

probes.query: How long did the resuscitation attempt last that night?
The grief of that night shift still surfaces when the ward goes quiet.
What happened before I could leave the parking lot sits in my chest and returns in the small hours.

episodes.text: The tomato seedlings sprouted, five of six pots. Moved them to the sunny rail and set a watering alarm named after the head nurse, which she must never learn.

probes.query: How many of the six tomato pots sprouted?
I feel cheerful remembering the seedlings coming up on the balcony.
Small green things I coaxed toward the light visit my thoughts on long shifts.

episodes.text: Suit fitting for the wedding with my brother. The tailor pinned my sleeves while we argued about the playlist, and I caught him grinning at me in the mirror like when we were kids.

probes.query: What did my brother and I argue about during the suit fitting?
I feel fond warmth thinking back to that fitting with my brother.
Two grown boys turning young again while getting measured — it comes back to me.

episodes.text: Scheduling committee posted the summer roster: I pulled six weekend nights in July, the most on the team again. Wrote a complaint draft, saved it, did not send it.

probes.query: How many weekend nights did the summer roster give me in July?
I still feel resentful about how that summer roster fell on me.
The words I typed and never delivered about my hours keep knocking on my mind.

episodes.text: Deep-cleaned the apartment, descaled the showerhead, and rotated the mattress. Podcast marathon in the background, three episodes about deep-sea fish.

probes.query: What was the podcast marathon about while I cleaned?
I feel calm recalling that thorough cleaning day.
A full reset of my place from corner to corner floats back when clutter piles up.

episodes.text: The wedding. I walked my brother down, managed the rings, and my toast landed every laugh I hoped for. Mom held both our hands during the photos and would not let go.

probes.query: What did Mom do during the wedding photos?
I feel overwhelming happiness whenever the wedding day replays in my head.
The day I stood beside him in front of everyone glows in me and returns often.

episodes.text: After the reception I drove Mom home. She fell asleep at a red light holding the bouquet, and I took the long way so she could rest.

probes.query: What was Mom holding when she fell asleep in the car?
I feel gentle warmth remembering that drive home with Mom.
A detour I chose so someone could keep sleeping stays quietly with me.

episodes.text: New grad nurse shadowed me for her first code blue drill. She froze at the cart, so afterwards we ran the checklist together five times until her hands stopped hovering.

probes.query: How many times did the new nurse and I run the checklist together?
I feel steady satisfaction about mentoring her through that drill.
Teaching someone until the trembling faded returns to me on mentoring days.

episodes.text: First ripe tomato from the balcony, exactly the size of a golf ball. Sliced it in half, salted it, and sent my brother a photo captioned harvest festival.

probes.query: What caption did I send with the tomato photo?
I feel simple delight recalling that first tomato from the garden.
Something I grew from nothing finally ready to eat keeps making me smile inside.

episodes.text: Day off. Laundry line full by nine, bank errand done, and a haircut before lunch. Bought bird seed for the balcony feeder on the way back.

probes.query: What did I buy for the balcony feeder?
I feel content thinking about that unhurried day off.
A morning where every small task fit neatly before noon drifts back to me.
